reasoning: The finding is a false positive for three compounding reasons. First, create_radio_group (lines 186-203) is dead code: a codebase-wide search across all PHP files in the plugin finds zero callers; the function is defined but never invoked. Second, the output path when $output=true passes through wp_kses_wf (line 199), which calls wp_kses($html, $allowed_tags). This sanitizer strips unrecognized event-handler attributes (onX), so stored/reflected XSS via attribute injection of JavaScript handlers is blocked. Third, the <label> tag that wraps the radio inputs is not present in the $allowed_tags whitelist built in wp_kses_wf (input, textarea, select, option, optgroup, a, div, li, span, style, fieldset, link, form, script, table, canvas are listed; label is absent), meaning the outer wrapper would be stripped and the output would be malformed even before any XSS payload executes. Additionally, comparable functions that ARE called (create_select_options, create_toggle_switch) are always passed hardcoded constant arrays -- not user-controlled data -- confirming that $options arrays in this codebase pattern are developer-defined, not attacker-influenced. Reachability is zero: no call site exists, no user data flows into this function, and the wp_kses layer provides defence-in-depth even in a hypothetical future caller scenario.
